name: Henry Martyn
title: Missionary to India & Persia
bio: English Anglican priest, Cambridge scholar, and missionary who served as a chaplain in India before traveling into Persia. A brilliant mathematician and linguist, he translated the New Testament into Hindustani, Persian, and Arabic and labored especially to bring the gospel to Muslims.
faith: Martyn left academic honors and comfort for difficult missionary service, writing, 'Let me burn out for God.' His short life became one of the great examples of evangelical missionary zeal: disciplined scholarship placed under Christ, language learning pressed into gospel service, and costly witness among peoples with little access to Scripture.

sources.title: Henry Martyn — Wikipedia
sources.note: Biography of the Anglican missionary and Bible translator

sources.title: Martyn, Henry (1781–1812) — Boston University History of Missiology
sources.note: Missionary biography emphasizing his work in India and among Muslims

sources.title: The Life and Letters of Henry Martyn
sources.note: Classic account of Martyn's life, journals, letters, and missionary labors

tagline: Brilliant Cambridge scholar who burned out for God in India and Persia
